programmes.show_description: Cameras capture footage of millions of strange creatures invading a church and some wonder if it's evidence of a grisly buried secret. (S4, ep 4)
El Yunque National Forest: Few know that one of the US's most mysterious National Parks is in Puerto Rico - a place some believe is home to a creature known as the Chupacabra. (S1, ep 7)
Gettysburg National Military Park: Many believe Gettysburg National Military Park is haunted by its violent past - here, explore some incredible claims about the park's history. (S1, ep 8)
Triangle Terror: For years, the flying saucer has been synonymous with UFO terror. But is it still the spacecraft that frightens us the most? Or has fear taken a new shape? (S3, ep 12)
Earth Portals: Experts unravel the secrets of the universe's earth portals: shortcuts through space that allow travellers to span vast distances in the blink of an eye. (S3, ep 13)
Solar System UFOs: Discover the shocking truth about alien civilisations in our solar system. Check out derelict spacecrafts and explore the mysterious origins of UFOs. (S3, ep 14)
Nazi UFOs: Could alien technology have helped the Nazis' rise to power? Experts question whether Hitler had extraterrestrial help in his quest for world domination. (S3, ep 15)
Alien Bodies: Uncover the shocking truth about alien remains as experts investigate unsettling reports of extraterrestrial bodies recovered from crashed UFOs. (S3, ep 16)
UFO Zones: Journey to the world's hottest UFO zones, where physical and paranormal phenomenons and unidentified objects and aliens are reported with alarming frequency. (S3, ep 17)
Countdown To Disclosure: Evidence suggests the world's governments could be on the verge of announcing everything they know about UFOs. What will this mean for our society? (S3, ep 18)
Top Civilian Encounters: From mass sightings in the sky to reports of alien probes found in human bodies, explore the ten most disturbing extraterrestrial encounters ever. (S3, ep 19)
Houston, We Have A Problem: Drinks are thrown, fists fly and a car is rammed during an argument at an intersection. Plus, a lane change miscalculation turns into a nightmare. (S1, ep 14)
Lit Joyride: A motorcyclist in Colorado keeps his cool during a heated argument until the other driver touches his bike. A valet's joyride in Miami goes up in smoke. (S1, ep 15)
Inside Job: When the Golds uncover a series of bogus pawns, their investigation leads to a trusted employee and a confession. (S12, ep 8)
The Hot Rod: Les and Seth visit a customer with a garage full of amazing vintage cars. Against his kids' wishes, Les offers big money for one of the seller's golden oldies. (S12, ep 9)
Ashley in Charge: With Les and Seth away at the Autorama car show - and Ashley in charge of the store - the employees go a little crazy. (S12, ep 10)
Karen in the Middle: Karen tries to make peace between Seth and Ashley, Bobby J takes a two-storey tractor for a spin and a watch snob riles Seth. (S12, ep 11)
Million Dollar Deal Part 1: A ventriloquist leaves Karen and Bobby J speechless, while an angry mixed martial arts fighter takes a swing at Byron. (S12, ep 12)
Million Dollar Deal Part 2: The Golds' million dollar art deal takes more twists and turns, but eventually, Les shakes hands on the single biggest purchase in store history. (S12, ep 13)
Henry, Skid and Allen splash the fun fund on a forgotten Raleigh Grifter and race to revive it for one last big finish. Season finale. (S4, ep 10)
Triumph Herald: The team acquire a Triumph Herald that has only had two owners. If they can repair the engine, brakes and gears, they've got a gem on their hands. (S3, ep 11)
Darting and Dodging: Rick and Corey dart out of the shop to check out a 1968 convertible. Meanwhile, Chum desperately wants a piece of an oreodont fossil. (S24, ep 15)
Battle-Pawn: Rick prepares for battle over a pair of vintage posters, Corey takes a look at an old fireman's megaphone and Chum heads over to a photo gallery. (S24, ep 16)
The Big Score: A money-making mission to downtown LA leads Ton and Allen to a copper cash till and a rare pistol. But will their finds bring them big profits? (S1, ep 2)
Ton's Got a Gun: A bidding war results in a depression-era slot machine, a custom mini-bike and a 1880s Colt Peacemaker. But can Ton and Allen make their money back? (S1, ep 3)
Strat'ed for Cash: The guys head to a high-end auction, where they lay their hands on a rare Fender Stratocaster. Plus, can they turn a rusty 1936 Schwinn to riches? (S1, ep 4)
The Real Thing: Ton and Allen get a surprise when they win a classic GMC truck. They also find a first-of-its kind Coca-Cola vending machine. But is it the real McCoy? (S1, ep 5)
Weapons of Past Destruction: Ton and Allen find centuries-old Masonic swords and a Nixon-era lie detector. Plus, they test out Civil War muskets on the firing range. (S1, ep 6)
Gangster Whiskey: Ton discovers some 1920s prohibition whiskey that could have been smuggled by Al Capone. But will he sell it or swig it? (S1, ep 7)
Hestered in the Highlands: The buyers head to the hills of Murietta for an auction that has tensions and prices running high. Dave drives Darrell to breaking point. (S7, ep 10)
Auction Boogaloo: Fierce competition greets Rene and Casey as they return to the auctions. Dave adds to the heat before unearthing a fantastic piece of board game history. (S7, ep 11)
Locktoberfest: Darrell and Brandon have high hopes as they return to the site of their biggest score. The others bid to take down the self-proclaimed "King of Montebello". (S7, ep 12)
My Little Brony: The action shifts to Oceanside, where Rene plans to make the others pay for crashing his home turf. Darrell and Brandon fight for a piece of film history. (S7, ep 13)
Up The Ante In El Monte: Big lockers mean big money in El Monte where Brandi and Jarrod try to play the other buyers. But what happens if their scheme backfires? (S7, ep 14)
All Along The Swatchtower: Jarrod's antics push Brandi to her limits as the auctions move to Koreatown. With highend merchandise up for grabs, Dave intends to score big. (S7, ep 15)
Purgatory Bridge: Andy and Paul return to the Ranch, at the bridge that has been dubbed The Miller Triangle and find more evidence that the two sites are linked. (S3, ep 5)
Pushing Boundaries: When the team deploys a cutting-edge new technology on Skinwalker Ranch to study the mysterious Bubble, it leads to an unsettling revelation. (S6, ep 5)
The sinking of RMS Titanic was one of the 20th century's greatest tragedies - but was it also a crime? This film takes a fresh look at the case and examines recently discovered evidence.
Oceanos: After being foundered in a storm, Oceanos was abandoned by many of her crew - including the negligent captain - leaving hundreds of passengers stranded on board. (S2, ep 4)
The Ark of the Covenant: Investigate the theories behind where the Ark of the Covenant - an artefact thought to be the most sacred relic of the Israelites - may be hidden. (S4, ep 6)
A shadowy figure spies on a paranormal investigator near an abandoned mining tunnel and a man and his son stumble upon a very talkative Bigfoot. (S8, ep 3)
Orb & Crash Debris, KGB UFOs and Aliens, and Superhuman reflexes: Is the strange silver orb in the sky a UFO? And is an infamous spy organisation's footage of aliens real? (S4, ep 4)
Beastly Howls, Holloman Air Force Base UFO, and the Cabin That Defies Gravity: How does a cabin seemingly defy gravity? And what is the origin of some frightening howls? (S4, ep 5)
Worm in the Sky, Skeletal Mystery Remains and Thessaloniki Mystery Sounds: What causes the thunderous banging noises that wake Thessaloniki residents every night? (S4, ep 10)
Glasgow Age of Steam: Alice Roberts explores Glasgow's age of steam, from lethal diseases and industrial injury to genteel tea rooms. (S3, ep 5)
Georgian Edinburgh: Alice Roberts explores Georgian Edinburgh's finest architecture, the roots of modern Scottishness, and some dark imperial controversies. (S3, ep 6)
Chesterfield 3: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 30)
Chum Goes to Hollywood: Rick tackles a football helmet signed by 28 Heisman Trophy winners, while Corey gears up to buy a devastatingly cool 'Transformers' toy for the shop. (S24, ep 20)
...Henry's Wreck-trospective. Sam Stratford Wreck-Trospective: Dive headfirst into the archives to celebrate the very best bits from the hit series Junk & Disorderly. (S1, ep 10)
A San Marcos Mitzvah: The auction action moves to Darrell and Brandon's home turf, where Mary comes up with her own way to deal with inflated prices. (S7, ep 16)
North Hollywood Hustle: Even Dan and Laura fail to escape the fierce competition at locker auctions in North Hollywood. (S7, ep 17)
Dangerous Methods: Brandon and Lori go for the gold - but find something even better. (S2, ep 20)
Mother Truckers: Sean enters one of the most unusual, big money auctions yet, Scott and Jesse go head to head, Brandon and Lori take a risk and Papa Bear goes all in. (S2, ep 21)
Freak of the Deep: Deep-sea explorers discover a monster thousands of feet deep in the ocean, killer fog threatens a US town, and a truck spontaneously explodes on a freeway. (S2, ep 1)
Thousands of UFO sightings and abductions are reported every year. This series examines these accounts and casts new light on historic cases. (S1, ep 5)
Darting Around: When the team flies a cutting-edge radar device high above Skinwalker Ranch, it reveals shocking new clues about the Bubble and what may be inside the Mesa. (S6, ep 9)
Detective Rick Tracy: One seller hopes to score with his Magic Johnson birthday invitation, while Chum takes a look at joker playing cards used as props in The Dark Knight. (S24, ep 19)
Who Let the Daves Out?: Dave draws on the help of friends at vault auctions in Huntingdon Beach. Meanwhile, Mary's inexperience shows when she struggles to secure a lot. (S7, ep 19)
Lock & Roll: Ivy sets his sights on a locker load of collectibles at auctions in Mission Hills. Meanwhile, local buyers test Darrell and Mary hopes for a quick sale. (S7, ep 18)
Northern Lights. Northern Frights: The buyers travel to Aurora in search of treasure. Roy finds a hidden gem, while Rick and Cindy investigate a mysterious item. (S1, ep 1)
Orangeville Squeeze Play: In Orangeville, Roy bids up the competition, Ursula has something to prove and Paul and Bogart stumble across an intriguing item. (S1, ep 2)
The Top Ten Alien Disasters: Explore the most devastating disasters ever documented that may have an otherworldly connection, from a flying monster to a deadly virus. (S16, ep 6)
Anchorage: Expert Chris Rutkowski, home to the largest UFO research archive, sets the scene and explains an unexplained event. (S1, ep 4)
Dangerous Methods: Brandon and Lori go for the gold - but find something even better. (S2, ep 20)
Mother Truckers: Sean enters one of the most unusual, big money auctions yet, Scott and Jesse go head to head, Brandon and Lori take a risk and Papa Bear goes all in. (S2, ep 21)
A San Marcos Mitzvah: The auction action moves to Darrell and Brandon's home turf, where Mary comes up with her own way to deal with inflated prices. (S7, ep 16)
North Hollywood Hustle: Even Dan and Laura fail to escape the fierce competition at locker auctions in North Hollywood. (S7, ep 17)
Chesterfield 4: David Dickinson travels the country, team in tow, hoping to snap up some good deals. But will the locals take the cash for their antiques or gamble at auction? (S10, ep 31)
Posting Up: As the team conducts an operation to mark the barrier of the massive Bubble anomaly, they make a series of shocking discoveries. (S6, ep 6)
Pennsylvania's Roswell: When Andy and Paul investigate the site of a controversial 1965 UFO crash, they discover an entire region of high strangeness. (S3, ep 6)
Egypt's Elite: Using cutting-edge science, experts unravel the mysteries of Ancient Egypt's Elite, such as how Ramesses battled a biblical drought. (S2, ep 3)
